{"society_power": 3.7, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 0.2, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Curzon centralization and coercive famine/plague management", "reasoning": "Curzonian administrative consolidation, railways/telegraph expansion, and coercive plague and press measures deepened the Raj's despotic reach, raising state power to strong. Society gained ideological arsenal (drain theory, Dutt) and an \"extremist\" nationalist strand, but famine and plague mortality sapped mass capacity, so its rise was modest and it remained weak."}
